# 03 — User journeys

Three journeys are documented. Each is mapped to (a) trigger, (b) emotional state, (c) actions, (d) artifacts produced, (e) drop-off risk.

## Journey 1 — Discovery → first deployed page (self-serve, 30 min)

**Persona**: Maya (technical founder).
**Trigger**: She sees a Show HN comment that links to `landing-pages-on-demand.prin7r.com` with the line *"this entire site was generated by the product itself"*.

| Step | Surface         | Emotional state | Action                                                  | Artifact                |
|------|-----------------|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| 1    | HN tab          | curious + skeptical | Clicks link                                             | —                       |
| 2    | Pagewright landing  | scanning        | Reads hero (10s), spots the "press line" animation, scrolls | —                       |
| 3    | Pagewright landing  | reassured       | Sees the "all 20 sites you can click below were built by Pagewright" social proof grid | —                       |
| 4    | Brief form      | committed       | Fills in: business name, 1-paragraph description, target audience, primary CTA, brand vibe (3-tag picker) | brief draft             |
| 5    | Status page     | watchful        | Watches a 4-step progress (Brand → Copy → Press → Deploy) with live LLM excerpts | brief enqueued          |
| 6    | Email + status  | delighted       | Receives "your page is live at acme.com — preview link, edit-via-PR link, screenshot" | live URL                |

**Drop-off risk**: step 4 (brief form length). Mitigation: ≤5 fields visible at once; the form auto-saves; an "I'll just describe it in plain text" fallback throws the description into a single-pass extraction.

## Journey 2 — Recurring use (agency, weekly campaigns)

**Persona**: Daniel (fractional CMO, 6 portfolio clients).
**Trigger**: Wednesday standup — "we need three campaign landings by Friday for the back-to-school push."

| Step | Surface         | Emotional state | Action                                                  | Artifact                |
|------|-----------------|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| 1    | Pagewright dashboard | impatient   | Logs in, opens the workspace for client A               | brief history list      |
| 2    | New brief modal | efficient       | Selects "Use prior brand kit" (cached from last campaign), changes only the offer + headline | brief draft (forked)    |
| 3    | Status page     | parallel-tasking | Submits, opens two more tabs, repeats for clients B & C | 3 enqueued briefs       |
| 4    | Email digest    | confident       | All 3 live within 90 min; one digest with all 3 URLs    | 3 live URLs             |
| 5    | Slack channel   | accountable     | Forwards the digest to the client Slack room            | client-visible delivery |
| 6    | Dashboard       | tracking        | Comes back end-of-day to attach the Plausible widget    | analytics on attached   |

**Drop-off risk**: step 4 trust ("did they all really go live?"). Mitigation: the digest embeds a screenshot + Lighthouse score + curl status code per page.

## Journey 3 — Done-for-you premium (concierge tier)

**Persona**: a non-technical operator (e.g. a clinic owner) who can't fill a brief without conversation.
**Trigger**: Books a 20-min Calendly slot from the landing's "Talk to a human" CTA (priced higher tier).

| Step | Surface             | Emotional state | Action                                                  | Artifact                |
|------|---------------------|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| 1    | Calendly + Zoom     | nervous         | Joins the call                                          | call recording          |
| 2    | Live brief shared   | trusting        | Operator answers questions; concierge fills the brief on screen with the operator watching | brief                   |
| 3    | Live preview        | engaged         | Concierge submits; the status page renders inside the Zoom share | brief enqueued          |
| 4    | 30 min later        | impressed       | Page is live; concierge does one round of human polish (typography, photo swap, copy tone) | first-cut landing       |
| 5    | Calendly follow-up  | satisfied       | 48h later, concierge sends final URL + Loom walkthrough  | final landing + walkthrough |
| 6    | Invoice + retainer  | committed       | Operator pays $1,200 and signs into the recurring tier  | paid customer           |

**Drop-off risk**: step 4 trust gap (does the concierge polish actually happen?). Mitigation: every concierge job includes a Loom that *shows the human edits* in a side-by-side diff.

## Cross-journey instrumentation

| Event                  | Capture surface | Used for                              |
|------------------------|-----------------|---------------------------------------|
| `brief_started`        | landing form    | top-of-funnel CTR                     |
| `brief_submitted`      | API             | conversion rate / activation          |
| `press_completed`      | orchestrator    | unit-time-to-first-value              |
| `landing_live`         | deploy runner   | promise-keeping (target ≤30 min p95)  |
| `landing_first_visit`  | edge log        | post-delivery activation              |
| `re_press_requested`   | dashboard       | iteration loop health                 |
